User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the iykyk platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

bio.description: A short biography or description of the user.

isCreator.description: Indicates if the user is a creator with special permissions.

Venue.title: Venue
Venue.description: Represents a venue (e.g., restaurant, bar, fitness studio) in the iykyk platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the venue entity.

address.description: The street address of the venue.

latitude.description: The latitude coordinate of the venue.

longitude.description: The longitude coordinate of the venue.

category.description: The category of the venue (e.g., restaurant, bar).

Experience.title: Experience
Experience.description: Represents an experience or event happening at a venue.

id.description: Unique identifier for the experience entity.

startTime.description: The start time of the experience.

endTime.description: The end time of the experience.

moods.description: An array of moods associated with the experience.

Deal.title: Deal
Deal.description: Represents a deal or promotion offered at a venue or by a creator.

id.description: Unique identifier for the deal entity.

venueName.description: Denormalized name of the venue for efficient querying.

creatorId.description: Reference to User who created this deal. (Relationship: User 1:N Deal)

startTime.description: The start time of the deal.

endTime.description: The end time of the deal.

dealType.description: Used to differentiate between standard 'Deals' and real-time 'Fire' items.

Community.title: Community
Community.description: Represents a themed community within the iykyk platform (e.g., sushi lovers, cocktail hunters).

id.description: Unique identifier for the community entity.

category.description: The category of the community (e.g., food, drink, fitness).

SocialActivity.title: Social Activity
SocialActivity.description: Represents a real-time social activity or event created by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the social activity.

title.description: The title of the social activity (e.g., 'Spikeball at 4pm').

hostId.description: The ID of the user hosting the activity.

hostUsername.description: The denormalized username of the host for easy display.

locationText.description: A descriptive location for the activity (e.g., 'Middle of Bondi Beach').

time.description: The scheduled time for the activity.

maxParticipants.description: The maximum number of participants allowed.

currentParticipants.description: The current count of joined participants.

description.description: A brief description of the activity.

SocialParticipant.title: Social Participant
SocialParticipant.description: Represents a user's participation status in a social activity.

userId.description: The ID of the user joining or requesting to join.

status.description: The participation status of the user.

joinedAt.description: The timestamp when the user joined or requested.

ClaimedDeal.title: Claimed Deal
ClaimedDeal.description: A record of a user claiming a deal.

JoinedSocial.title: Joined Social
JoinedSocial.description: A record of a user joining a social activity.

Following.title: Following
Following.description: A record of a user following another user (creator).

creatorName.description: Denormalized creator name for efficient querying.

creatorAvatarUrl.description: Denormalized creator avatar URL for efficient querying.

SavedStay.title: Saved Stay
SavedStay.description: A record of a user saving a stay for later.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Path-based ownership for user-specific data.
Tracks deals claimed by the user.
Tracks social activities the user has joined.
Tracks other users (creators) this user is following.
Tracks stays the user has saved.
Stores venue information. Top-level entity for easy querying.
Stores experiences associated with a venue.

A top-level collection for all deals, fire items, and perks. This allows for efficient global queries, like finding all active 'fire' deals, without needing to query every venue subcollection. Denormalized data like venueName is included to support this.
Stores community information. Includes a 'members' map (uid: role) for role-based access control.
A top-level collection for user-created social activities. Allows for easy querying of all active events.
Manages participants for a specific social activity. The document ID is the user's ID for easy lookup.
A top-level collection for all creator-recommended stays and flash stays. Allows for easy querying of all available accommodations.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support iykyk's real-time cultural portal concept. It prioritizes efficient querying for features like the 'Fire' feed by using a top-level denormalized '/deals' collection. A new top-level '/socials' collection is added for real-time user-generated events, separating them from long-term themed communities. This allows for efficient querying of all active social gatherings. Subcollections are used for managing participants in a scalable way.

**Authorization Independence (Denormalization):**

*   Authorization is implemented using path-based ownership for private user data and membership maps for collaborative data. Where access to a subcollection depends on attributes of a parent collection, these attributes are denormalized into the subcollection documents. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules, promoting atomic operations and simplifying debugging.

**QAPs (Queryability and Security):**

*   **Segregation:** Data with different security requirements is stored in separate collections. For example, user-owned data resides under `/users/{userId}`, while venue and community data are stored in top-level collections with appropriate security rules.
*   **Membership Maps:** Communities utilize a `members` map to manage user roles, enabling secure listing of community members and controlling access to community-specific data.

**Explanation of Collections:**

*   `/users/{userId}`: Stores user profiles, providing a dedicated path for user-specific data and simplifying ownership-based security rules. User activity like claimed deals and joined socials are tracked in subcollections for scalability.
*   `/venues/{venueId}`: Stores venue information as top-level entities for easy querying.
*   `/venues/{venueId}/experiences/{experienceId}`: Stores experiences associated with a venue. The `venueId` is denormalized for authorization independence.
*   `/deals/{dealId}`: A denormalized, top-level collection for all deals and 'Fire' items. This is crucial for the performance of the 'iykyk Fire' feed, allowing a single query to fetch all relevant, time-sensitive items across all venues.
*   `/communities/{communityId}`: Stores community information, with a `members` map for role-based access control.
*   `/socials/{socialId}`: A new top-level collection for user-created, real-time social activities ('Squads'), allowing for efficient global queries on active events.
*   `/socials/{socialId}/participants/{userId}`: A subcollection to manage requests and joined members for a social activity, enabling clear security rules for joining and viewing participants.
